content.text: I'll start by reading the test file and implementation file to understand the current state.
Now let me run the tests to confirm the current failure.

input.description: Run tests to see current state

content.text:
Good. The failure is confirmed: 155 instead of 125. The current logic only handles the case where ALL components are the same type and there are exactly 3. With 3 runes + 3 moonstones (6 components total, mixed types), it falls through to the `else` branch and prices each individually at 25 G = 150 + 5 = 155.

The minimal fix is to group components by type, and for each group of exactly 3, apply the block premium (60 G), otherwise price individually.

content.text: All 13 tests pass, 31 remain as todo. Let me verify the final state of the implementation file.

**Over-implementation check:**
- Did I implement features for future tests? No. The grouping-by-type logic is the minimum needed to handle the case where multiple component types each independently form blocks.
- Did I add logic not demanded by the current test? No. The previous tests (1 rune, 2 runes, 3 runes, 4 runes, 7 runes, 2 runes + 1 moonstone) all still pass because the grouping logic correctly handles single-type groups as well.
- Did I optimize prematurely? No.
- Did I refactor existing code? I replaced the old conditional with the grouped approach, which is the minimal structural change needed to make this test pass. The old conditional could not handle mixed-type blocks.

---

Green Phase Complete:

**Implementation**: Replaced the single-conditional block-pricing logic (lines 43-49) with a group-by-type approach. Components are grouped into a `Record<string, any[]>` by their `type`. For each group, if it has exactly 3 items (`BLOCK_SIZE`), the 60 G block premium is applied; otherwise each item is priced individually at 25 G.

**Result**: All 13 tests now pass (31 remaining as todo).

**Approach**: This is the minimal change required. The previous logic checked whether ALL components were the same type AND numbered exactly 3 -- it could not handle the case of two separate same-type groups each forming their own block. Grouping by type and evaluating each group independently is the simplest way to satisfy both the new test (3 runes + 3 moonstones = two blocks = 120 G) and all existing tests.

**Files modified**: `/home/experimenter/experiments/runs/2026-05-21_15-29-15_claim-office-example-mapping_v4.1-testlist-scope-fix_opus-4-6-portkey-no-thinking-3/src/claim-office.ts` (lines 43-57)

Proceeding to Refactor phase.
